headline: Ford, Applied Digital, U Power, Block And Alphabet: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
summary: U.S. stock markets experienced a significant surge on Wednesday, following a mixed close the previous day. The futures of major benchmark indices climbed as President Donald Trump declared a two-week ceasefire on Iran strikes. The Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 2.85% to 47,909.92, while the S&P 500 climbed 2.5% to 6,782.81 and the Nasdaq advanced 2.8% to 22,634.99. These are the top stocks that gained the attention of retail traders and investors through the day. Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F)

headline: AI Makes Strange Bedfellows of Ford and GE Aerospace. Here’s Why It Matters.
summary: Ford Motor and GE Aerospace are using artificial intelligence to improve efficiency and margins, highlighting why manufacturing companies may be less vulnerable to AI disruption than software firms.

headline: Crude Dips On Trump's Ceasefire, But Inflation Triggers A Consumer Shock: This Week On Wall Street
summary: Oil crashed 13% weekly on Iran ceasefire hopes before tensions reignited. Inflation spiked, gas prices surged, and U.S. consumer confidence hit a record low as markets rallied.

headline: What GM, Ford lose when their logos disappear from high-profile sites

headline: Ford Recasts EV Plans While Managing Recall And New Manufacturing Role
summary: Ford Motor (NYSE:F) disclosed sharp ongoing financial losses in its electric vehicle unit and announced a reset toward hybrids and more affordable EVs, including canceled models and a major writedown. The company is scaling a manufacturing partnership with Sharrow Engineering that materially shortens production timelines for new marine propeller technology. Ford also issued a large recall affecting more than 420,000 vehicles due to a windshield wiper defect with safety implications. For you...
